note: expected UTC values are computed from the tzdata above at generation time; regenerate after a tzdata upgrade (TZ=UTC python -m testpack.make_instants)

instants.why: spring-forward gap: this wall time does not exist; naive localization silently fabricates an instant — code must raise or shift per an explicit policy

fall-back overlap: this wall time happens twice; code must pick a policy (earlier/later) explicitly — pytz is_dst=False selects STANDARD time (the later occurrence), a documented misconception

23:00 the night before spring-forward + '4 wall hours later' is only 3 absolute hours; wall arithmetic and instant arithmetic diverge
Apia skipped 2011-12-30 entirely (day does not exist in this zone)
US DST began first-Sunday-of-April before 2007: 2006-03-12 (the 'modern' second-Sunday-of-March date) was NOT a transition in 2006; the real 2006 spring-forward was 2006-04-02
2100 is NOT a leap year (divisible by 100, not by 400) — naive %4 checks fail
Jan 31 + 1 month: policy must be pinned (clip to Feb 29/28 vs overflow to Mar) — library defaults differ
pre-1970 instants: floor vs trunc division diverges on negatives
